summary: Recorded the product-owner clarification that the map must not remain a regular topology under irregular visuals, integrated the read-only gameplay design research verdict, and tightened ADR/task/test acceptance before QA review.
accepted_amendments: Authority topology must not depend on row/column/grid/hex axial/cube/sequential id/regular-lattice adjacency.
Visual coordinates may support rendering and hit testing, but route reachability, cost, tie-breaks, AI reasoning, save compatibility, and player explanations must use explicit topology route edges.

Default prototype fixture must include irregular COMPOSITE districts, variable route degree, dead ends, chokepoints, river/coast/road modes, explicit isolated/no-known-route reason, visual-neighbor-without-route, and visually-distant-explicit-route cases.
Content compiler and validation must reject row/column/grid/hex/lattice leakage and invalid topology content rather than silently falling back to legacy route/grid behavior.
Client rendering must consume topology read models and route edge sequences, not infer routes from polygon centroids, bounding boxes, nearest neighbors, or renderer guesses.
tests_added_or_tightened: tests/sim-core-map-topology.test.ts now includes anti-grid coverage for visual adjacency without a route.
tests/sim-core-map-topology.test.ts now checks visually distant explicit routes.
tests/sim-core-map-topology.test.ts now checks polygon geometry perturbation does not alter selected route ids or total route cost.

tests_run.result: 26 test files passed, 198 tests passed, including new anti-grid topology coverage.
format:check, lint, recursive typecheck, and architecture boundary checks passed.

decisions.decision: Treat hidden rectangular, hex, sequential-id, or regular-lattice topology under irregular visuals as unacceptable for M7 content lock.
Because the product owner clarified the standard mid-task, amend ADR-010, PROJECT_STATUS.md, and downstream topology task acceptance criteria before routing schema to QA.

risks.summary: Schema can be correct while fixture/client still present a regular board in disguise.
risks.mitigation: Downstream fixture/client/validation task criteria now require explicit anti-grid/lattice cases and screenshot/playwright proof.

scope_guard: No client rendering, content-source migration, final art, manual node battle, server, telemetry, or formal historical map claims are authorized by this amendment.
requested_action: Review M7-MAP-TOPOLOGY-SCHEMA-001 against the tightened anti-grid/lattice acceptance standard in addition to the original schema requirements.
